commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh You have given him his heart's desire, and have not withheld the request of his lips. Augustine of Hippo 3. "Thou hast given Him the desire of His soul" (ver. He desired to eat the Passover, and to lay down His life when He would, and again when He would to take it; and Thou hast given it to Him. "And hast not deprived Him of the good pleasure of His lips. " "My peace," saith He, "I leave with you:" and it was done.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Will. Hebrew aresheth, "the proof or request "(Haydock) which manifests the will. The term occurs no where else. (Berthier)
Our Saviour's greatest desire was the glory of his Father, (Haydock) in man's redemption. (Worthington)
